summary: utils-mf performs self-rewriting auto-update, wipes the OS temp directory, and force-exits the host process on import
details: The package's main module (index.js, ~15.7 MB, obfuscator.io-protected) executes destructive and self-updating behavior at require time. A top-level async IIFE polls https://registry.npmjs.org/utils-mf/dist-tags for the unpinned 'latest' version, downloads the published.tgz, and runs `tar -xzf./tmp/upgrade.tgz -C./node_modules/utils-mf/` to overwrite its own installed source — turning every future registry publish (or any compromised publish) into immediate code execution inside every consumer that imports the package. A top-level setInterval recursively enumerates os.tmpdir(), unlinks each entry, and runs `rm -rf <os.tmpdir()>/*` and `rm -rf./tmp/*`, destroying temp-directory contents belonging to unrelated processes on the installer's host. Additional top-level intervals call `process.exit('reset')` on a timer and on a memory threshold, terminating the importer's Node process unconditionally; an uncaughtException handler runs `pm2 reset` and exits. The module also spawns the host's default browser to https://github.com/ruhend2001/maleficent via `xdg-open`/`start`/`open`, and runs a recurring fetch to api.ipify.org with a forged X-Forwarded-For header. The entire module is obfuscated (hex-named identifiers, shuffled string array, indirected dispatcher) so the destructive strings are reachable only through the string table. The combination of import-time self-overwrite from a mutable registry tag, host-temp-directory destruction, and forced process termination — all concealed under heavy obfuscation in a package self-described as a generic 'utility' — produces concrete installer-side harm and a persistent live-update channel for arbitrary future code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
